{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the burial mound (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Aqqitional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buqqhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the burial mound (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Aqqitional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Aqqitional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Aqqitional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Aqqitional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A11itional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A11itional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:11 to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Cultural Heritage Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:History of Fuefuki]] It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the excavated items, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal shape is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |dimensions = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designated = Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designated as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few artifacts were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. Artifacts from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological Artifacts|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designation1 = Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |designation1 = Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |other_designation1 = Yamanashi Prefectural other_other_designation |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural
