revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |other_designation1 = Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |other_designation1 = Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |other_designation1 = Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |other_designation1 = Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is other_designation1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural other_designation.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == other_designation == === Yamanashi Prefectural {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. A[[Category:dd to dd]]dditional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kyozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳]] [[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kyozuka Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川|12=draft}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=draft}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=draft}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=draft}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=draft}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=draft}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=draft}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=draft}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=draft}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市|12=draft}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kyozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳]] [[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kyozuka Kofun}}

During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kyozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳]] [[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kyozuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川|12=draft}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=draft}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=draft}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=draft}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=draft}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=draft}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=draft}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=draft}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=draft}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市|12=draft}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kyozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳]] [[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kyozuka Kofun}}

It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川|12=draft}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=draft}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=draft}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=draft}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=draft}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=draft}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=draft}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=draft}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=draft}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市|12=draft}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kyozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳]] [[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kyozuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=draft}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=draft}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市|12=draft}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kyozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳]] [[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kyozuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Kanagawa River|ja|金川|12=draft}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=draft}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=draft}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=draft}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=draft}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=draft}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=draft}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=draft}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=draft}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Imperial mausoleums|ja|天皇陵|1=Japanese imperial tombs|12=draft}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Tama City|ja|多摩市|12=draft}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Fujioka|ja|藤岡市|12=draft}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:dd to dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Octagonal tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kyozuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳]] [[sn:Kyozuka Kofun]] {{citation needed|Kyozuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=経塚古墳 |所在地=[[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]一宮町国分字経塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[八角墳]] |築造時期=[[7世紀]]前半 |規模=対辺長12.5[[メートル|m]]
高さ2.2m |被葬者= |出土品=鉄斧 |史跡指定=山梨県指定史跡「経塚古墳」 |画像=[[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
墳丘（2010年7月撮影） |地図2={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''経塚古墳'''（きょうづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]一宮町国分にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。山梨県指定史跡に指定されている[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 経塚古墳] 山梨県サイト。 == 概要 == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室内部}}]] [[甲府盆地]]東部、旧一宮町西部の[[笛吹川]]支流金川[[扇状地]]上に位置。標高は348[[メートル|m]]。周辺には[[古墳時代後期]]から終末期の国分古墳群が分布し、続く[[奈良時代]]移行の[[甲斐国分寺]]跡や[[甲斐国分尼寺]]跡も見られる。古墳の存在はそれまで知られており、6～7世紀の小型[[円墳]]と推定されていたが、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）に水害防備保安林を[[森林公園]]として整備する際に[[山梨県教育委員会]]による調査が実施され、全国で9例目、東日本では3例目にあたる八角墳と確認された。 [[発掘調査]]により[[石室]]は全面解体され、復元整備が施された。出土遺物は少なく、築造期の鉄[[斧]]のほかは二次的な[[甲斐型土器|甲斐型坏]]や人骨が見られるのみ。古墳の規模は直径12.5m、高さ2.2m、[[横穴式石室]]を有する。築造年代は近辺の[[終末期古墳]]との類似や石室構造、出土遺物の鉄斧から古墳時代終末期の7世紀前半と推定された。出土遺物は[[山梨県埋蔵文化センター]]に保管され、古墳は山梨県森林公園金川の森として整備が完了している。 八角墳は古墳の形態変化の過程で生じたものではなく、思想的影響であるものと考えられている。[[八角形]]が鎮魂の意味を持ち寺院建築にも応用されている[[仏教]]思想や、[[天子]]の祭礼や[[皇帝]]礼儀の象徴的意味を持つ[[中国思想]]的宇宙観の影響によるものであると考えられており、日本では[[畿内]]での[[天皇陵]]のほかは西日本では[[兵庫県]]、東日本では[[東京都]][[多摩市]]の[[稲荷塚古墳]]（7世紀前半）、[[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]の[[伊勢塚古墳 (藤岡市)|伊勢塚古墳]]（[[6世紀]]前半）、[[北群馬郡]][[吉岡町]]の[[三津屋古墳]]（7世紀前半）の例があるのみであった。 山梨県内では盆地南端の[[曾根丘陵]]に分布する古墳群のうち、4世紀後半の[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]を中心とした畿内色の強い大型古墳が分布し、早くから畿内王権との関わりが指摘されていた。やがて盆地各地に大型古墳を築造する勢力が出現し、奈良時代には盆地東部の豪族が新たに畿内王権との関わりをもち、政治的中心地も遷っている。経塚古墳をはじめ関東での八角墳の例により、それまで[[皇族]]など最高首長層に限られていた古墳時代の墓制が東国でも採用されていたことが明らかになった。 1994年（平成6年）に実施された石室の復元工事では[[クレーン]]などの重機を用いず、山梨県内で「かぐらさん」と呼称される木製の[[巻上機]]を用いて石室の解体作業を実施した。 == 文化財 == === 山梨県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 経塚古墳 - 1994年（平成6年）11月7日指定。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *吉岡弘樹「経塚古墳」『山梨県資料編1原始・古代〔考古（遺跡）〕』（1998） == 関連項目 == * [[姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 経塚古墳] - 山梨県ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:きようつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:笛吹市の歴史]] [[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=経塚古墳 |所在地=[[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]一宮町国分字経塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|scale:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |形状=[[八角墳]] |築造時期=[[7世紀]]前半 |規模=対辺長12.5[[メートル|m]]
高さ2.2m |被葬者= |出土品=鉄斧 |史跡指定=山梨県指定史跡「経塚古墳」 |画像=[[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
墳丘（2010年7月撮影） |地図2={{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''経塚古墳'''（きょうづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]一宮町国分にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[八角墳]]。山梨県指定史跡に指定されている[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 経塚古墳] 山梨県サイト。 == 概要 == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室内部}}]] [[甲府盆地]]東部、旧一宮町西部の[[笛吹川]]支流金川[[扇状地]]上に位置。標高は348[[メートル|m]]。周辺には[[古墳時代後期]]から終末期の国分古墳群が分布し、続く[[奈良時代]]移行の[[甲斐国分寺]]跡や[[甲斐国分尼寺]]跡も見られる。古墳の存在はそれまで知られており、6～7世紀の小型[[円墳]]と推定されていたが、[[1994年]]（[[平成]]6年）に水害防備保安林を[[森林公園]]として整備する際に[[山梨県教育委員会]]による調査が実施され、全国で9例目、東日本では3例目にあたる八角墳と確認された。 [[発掘調査]]により[[石室]]は全面解体され、復元整備が施された。出土遺物は少なく、築造期の鉄[[斧]]のほかは二次的な[[甲斐型土器|甲斐型坏]]や人骨が見られるのみ。古墳の規模は直径12.5m、高さ2.2m、[[横穴式石室]]を有する。築造年代は近辺の[[終末期古墳]]との類似や石室構造、出土遺物の鉄斧から古墳時代終末期の7世紀前半と推定された。出土遺物は[[山梨県埋蔵文化センター]]に保管され、古墳は山梨県森林公園金川の森として整備が完了している。 八角墳は古墳の形態変化の過程で生じたものではなく、思想的影響であるものと考えられている。[[八角形]]が鎮魂の意味を持ち寺院建築にも応用されている[[仏教]]思想や、[[天子]]の祭礼や[[皇帝]]礼儀の象徴的意味を持つ[[中国思想]]的宇宙観の影響によるものであると考えられており、日本では[[畿内]]での[[天皇陵]]のほかは西日本では[[兵庫県]]、東日本では[[東京都]][[多摩市]]の[[稲荷塚古墳]]（7世紀前半）、[[群馬県]][[藤岡市]]の[[伊勢塚古墳 (藤岡市)|伊勢塚古墳]]（[[6世紀]]前半）、[[北群馬郡]][[吉岡町]]の[[三津屋古墳]]（7世紀前半）の例があるのみであった。 山梨県内では盆地南端の[[曾根丘陵]]に分布する古墳群のうち、4世紀後半の[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]を中心とした畿内色の強い大型古墳が分布し、早くから畿内王権との関わりが指摘されていた。やがて盆地各地に大型古墳を築造する勢力が出現し、奈良時代には盆地東部の豪族が新たに畿内王権との関わりをもち、政治的中心地も遷っている。経塚古墳をはじめ関東での八角墳の例により、それまで[[皇族]]など最高首長層に限られていた古墳時代の墓制が東国でも採用されていたことが明らかになった。 1994年（平成6年）に実施された石室の復元工事では[[クレーン]]などの重機を用いず、山梨県内で「かぐらさん」と呼称される木製の[[巻上機]]を用いて石室の解体作業を実施した。 == 文化財 == === 山梨県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 経塚古墳 - 1994年（平成6年）11月7日指定。 ==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *吉岡弘樹「経塚古墳」『山梨県資料編1原始・古代〔考古（遺跡）〕』（1998） == 関連項目 == * [[姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 経塚古墳] - 山梨県ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:きようつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:笛吹市の歴史]] [[Category:八角墳]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the [[:en:Jinchuan|Kanagawa River]], Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[:en:1994|1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|qq=|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|qq=|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}}

View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the [[:en:Jinchuan|Kanagawa River]], Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters.
