While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the [[:en:Jinchuan|Kanagawa River]], Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[:en:1994|1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}}

The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the [[:en:Jinchuan|Kanagawa River]], Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[:en:1994|1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[:en:1994|1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the [[:en:Jinchuan|Kanagawa River]], Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[:en:1994|1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the [[:en:Jinchuan|Kanagawa River]], Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[:en:1994|1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the [[:en:Jinchuan|Kanagawa River]], Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[:en:1994|1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the [[:en:Jinchuan|Kanagawa River]], Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in [[:en:1994|1994]] (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]] in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Tama, Tokyo|Tama City]], Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Fujioka, Gunma|Fujioka]], Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Jinchuan|en|Jinchuan|WD=Q358450|lt=Kanagawa River}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki, Yamanashi}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Jinchuan|en|Jinchuan|WD=Q358450|lt=Kanagawa River}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}} (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki, Yamanashi|1=Fuefuki}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki, Yamanashi|1=Fuefuki}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Jinchuan|en|Jinchuan|WD=Q358450|lt=Kanagawa River|1=Jinchuan}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}} (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City|1=Tama, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Kyozuka Kofun]][[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kyozuka Kofun |location = {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki, Yamanashi|1=Fuefuki}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan |coordinates = {{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E|map_size:20000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type = {{ill|Octagonal tumulus|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |period = Early [[7th century]] |area = 12.5m on each side; 2.2m in height |Historic Site1 = Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site |image = [[File: Kyozuka-kofun Old tomb at Fuefuki-city.JPG|250px]]
View of the Kofun (July 2010) |map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|38|20.2|N|138|40|38.9|E}}}} }} '''Kyozuka Kofun''' (経塚古墳) is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki|en|Fuefuki|WD=Q849434|lt=Fuefuki, Yamanashi|1=Fuefuki}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan. It is Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site.[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefectural Website == Overview == [[File:Kyozuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Interior of the stone chamber}}]] Located in the eastern part of the [[Kōfu Basin]] on the alluvial fan of the {{ill|Jinchuan|en|Jinchuan|WD=Q358450|lt=Kanagawa River|1=Jinchuan}}, Kyozuka Kofun lies at an elevation of 348 meters. The surrounding area contains the remnants of the {{ill|Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja|国分古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, dating from the late Kofun period to the terminal Kofun period, as well as the sites of the [[Kai Kokubunji|Kai Provincial Temple]] and {{ill|Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja|甲斐国分尼寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Provincial Nunnery|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} from the subsequent Nara period. Previously believed to be a small circular tomb from the 6th to 7th century, the kofun was reclassified as an octagonal tumulus during excavations conducted in {{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}} (Heisei 6) by the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|ja|山梨県教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|1=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469676}}. This makes it one of only nine such examples nationwide and the third known in eastern Japan. The excavation revealed a {{ill|yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (horizontal stone chamber), which was fully dismantled and restored. Few burial goods were recovered, with the only notable find being an iron axe from the tumulus's construction period. Additional items include {{ill|Kai-style pottery|ja|甲斐型土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai-style pottery|1=Kai-type pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577873}} and human remains, which are believed to be secondary deposits. The tumulus measures 12.5 meters in diameter and 2.2 meters in height. Based on the chamber's structure and the burial goods, it is dated to the early 7th century. burial goods from the kofun are preserved at the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化センター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Center for Archaeological burial goods|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the site has been incorporated into the {{ill|Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja|森林公園金川の森|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanegawa-no-Mori Forest Park|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The octagonal type is thought to reflect ideological influences, including {{ill|Buddhist cosmology|ja|仏教宇宙論|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buddhist cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where the octagon symbolizes repose for the soul, as well as {{ill|Chinese cosmology|ja|中国思想|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinese cosmology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中国哲学|1=Chinese philosophy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q184663}}, representing imperial rites and the order of the universe. While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|WD=Q213263|lt=Tama City|1=Tama, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|WD=Q596486|lt=Fujioka|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, Gunma Prefecture. During the tumulus restoration in 1994, traditional wooden winches, locally known as "kagurasan," were used instead of modern heavy machinery. == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Kyozuka Kofun''' - Designated on November 7, 1994. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kyozuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#SHI0032 Yamanashi Historic Site Guide: Historic Sites - Kyozuka Kofun] - Yamanashi Prefecture Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] {{translated page|ja|経塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=88847674|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11606412}} [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

While this tomb type was predominantly used for {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|qid=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs|1=Japanese imperial tombs}} in western Japan, it is rare in eastern Japan, with examples including the {{ill|Inarizuka Kofun|ja|稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Tama, Tokyo|en|Tama, Tokyo|qid=Q213263|lt=Tama City|1=Tama, Tokyo}}, Tokyo, and the {{ill|Isezuka Kofun|ja|伊勢塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isezuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Fujioka, Gunma|en|Fujioka, Gunma|qid=Q596486|lt=Fujioka|1=Fujioka, Gunma}}, Gunma Prefecture.
